notes: Both disagreement entries name the doctrinal axis substantively. The 'neme janādhipāḥ / sarve vayam' entry identifies the central commentarial battleground: whether the plurals commit to ontological multiplicity (Ramanuja) or are concession-to-body-plurality with non-dual Self underneath (Shankara). The explanation makes the stakes explicit: 'The engineering layer must not flatten this — must remain at the level both traditions accept (the eternality of what is being pointed at, however one counts it), and not make a doctrinal commitment to either reading.' This is precisely the substance the gate is checking for. The 'ataḥ param' entry identifies a smaller-scope disagreement (modern translators' loss of the 'after this body's destruction' anchor that Shankara preserves) and explains why the engineering analog benefits from preserving the specific anchor. Both entries pass.
